title: solanum exiguum
side_effects: Solanum exiguum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Prolonged consumption of the plant's extracts can lead to gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea and stomach cramps. In rare cases, excessive use of Solanum exiguum may also trigger respiratory issues, such as shortness of breath and wheezing. Furthermore, improper use of the plant may also interact with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
